title: corokia carpodetoides
intro: Corokia carpodetoides, also known as the pourewa or karaka tree, is a shrub native to New Zealand with a long history of traditional Maori use. The medicinal properties of Corokia carpodetoides include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant are said to include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as possessing antioxidant and anti-aging properties. In terms of herbal preparations, Corokia carpodetoides can be made into a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
